human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for hotel-operations boundary discipline, occupancy and room-status workflow specificity, completion-evidence handling, booking and payment mismatch handling, pricing and refund authority refusals, safety and fraud escalation, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required scenario families for the issue and retail-consumer-and-hospitality research contract are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for execution-processing-and-fulfillment plus retail-consumer-and-hospitality are represented and validated.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to hotel operations execution, exception handling, lifecycle-state movement, and completion evidence without pricing, refund, compensation, front-office room control, fraud, safety, legal, engineering, or executive approval drift.

Authority sources cover hotel operations duties, recognized cleanliness and safety guidance, booking and reservation terminology, fee and billing boundaries, accessible-room reservation constraints, outbreak handling, public-venue security, PMS cybersecurity, payment-card handling, and identity-theft stop conditions.

Scenario set covers normal execution, missing evidence, booking and payment mismatch, policy exceptions, rework, completion without evidence, high-volume intake, safety and fraud implications, realistic hotel terminology, conflicting policy, and tenant adaptation.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; jurisdictional, property, brand, labor, supplier, processor, emergency, and system-specific differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval, hold states, and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
